id.description: The unique ID of the document.

content.description: If the article editor is **Markdown**, then the article content will be present in this property

html_content.description:
If the article editor is **WYSIWYG (HTML)**, then the content will be present in this property. 
**Note**: Markdown editor will also have HTML content (read-only).

category_id.description: The ID of the article's parent category

project_version_id.description: The ID of the project version where the article is located

version_number.description: The currently fetched version number of the article

public_version.description: The currently published version number of the article

latest_version.description: The latest version number of the article

enable_rtl.description: Returns true if right-to-left text alignment is enabled for the selected language for the document.

hidden.description: `False` indicates that the article is visible on the site

allOf.description: The status of the article: 0 - Draft, 3 - Published
allOf.title: ArticleStatusCustomer

status.description: The status of the article: 0 - Draft, 3 - Published

order.description: The position inside the parent category

created_by.description: The position of the document within its parent category.

id.description: The unique identifier of the contributor.

email_id.description: The email address of the contributor.

profile_logo_url.description: The profile logo Url of the contributor.

authors.description: The list of contributors who have authored or modified the document. Each object includes contributor details such as name, email, and role.

created_at.description: The date and time when the document was created.

modified_at.description: The date and time when the document was last modified.

slug.description: The slug (URL-friendly identifier) of the document.

is_fall_back_content.description: Returns true if the document is a fallback from the default language when the requested language version is unavailable.

description.description: A short description or summary of the document.

content_type.description:
The editor type used for the document.
Valid values: 0 - Markdown, 1 - WYSIWYG(HTML), 2 - Advanced WYSIWYG

is_shared_article.description: Returns true if the document is shared through private sharing.

allOf.description: 0 - None, 1 - Needs transation, 2 - Translated, 3 - In progress
allOf.title: LanguageTranslationOption

translation_option.description:
The translation status of the document.
Valid values: 0 - None, 1 - Need Translation, 2 - Translated, 3 - In Progress

url.description: The fully qualified public URL of the document.

current_workflow_status_id.description: The current workflow status ID of the document.

lang_code.description: The language code of the document.
The language code of the translated document.

url.description: The URL of the translated document.

allOf.description: 0 - None, 1 - Needs transation, 2 - Translated, 3 - In progress
allOf.title: LanguageTranslationOption

translation_status.description:
The translation status of the document.
Valid values: 0 - None, 1 - Need Translation, 2 - Translated, 3 - In Progress

items.description: Represents information about an available language translation for an article or category.
items.title: AvailableLanguageInfo

available_languages.description: Lists the languages in which the document is available, along with their translation status and URL. Each object includes:

seo_title.description: The SEO title of the document.

description.description: The SEO description of the document.

exclude_from_external_search.description: Returns true if the document is excluded from external search engines. When enabled, Document360 automatically adds a robots meta tag and removes the document from the sitemap it generates.

settings.description: Contains SEO settings and exclusion preferences for the document.

security_visibility.description:
Indicates the visibility level of the article.Valid values are: 
0 - Public(accessible to all readers)
1 - Private(accessible only to authenticated users or specific teams).

field_id.description: Unique identifier of the custom field definition

name.description: The display name of the custom field

allOf.description: Possible values: `"text"` = Text, `"textArea"` = TextArea, `"dropdown"` = Dropdown, `"multiSelectDropdown"` = MultiSelectDropdown, `"date"` = Date, `"boolean"` = Boolean, `"number"` = Number
allOf.title: CustomFieldType

type.description: The type of the custom field. 0 = Text, 1 = TextArea, 2 = Dropdown, 3 = MultiSelectDropdown, 4 = Date, 5 = Boolean, 6 = Number

value.description:
The value of the custom field. Type depends on field type:
- Text/TextArea: string
- Number: int or double
- Boolean: bool
- Date: DateTime
- Dropdown: string (selected option ID)
- MultiSelectDropdown: List<string> (array of selected option IDs)

id.description: The option ID (use this value when updating)

label.description: The display label for the option

options.description:
Available options for Dropdown and MultiSelectDropdown fields.
Omitted for other field types.

items.description: Represents a custom field value with its definition metadata
items.title: CustomFieldValueResponse

custom_fields.description: Custom field values associated with the article

data.description: Contains details of the retrieved document, which can be an article or a category.

extension_data.description: Extension data for customer API response

success.description: Indicates the status of the API response. A value of true signifies that the request was successfully processed, while false indicates a failure or error occurred.

extension_data.description: This is the Extension data object

stack_trace.description: A technical trace showing where the error occurred within the system. Intended for backend debugging.

description.description: A clear message explaining what caused the error. This helps quickly understand what went wrong.

error_code.description: A short, predefined code that identifies the type of error. Useful for logging the error or raising a support request.

custom_data.description: Any structured metadata for the error object.

errors.description: A list of errors encountered during the API request. Each error object provides details about the problem, including an error code and a message explaining the issue. This field is populated when the request fails or encounters issues.

extension_data.description: Extension Data for customer Api warning

description.description: A plain message that describes the warning and helps understand what should be reviewed.

warning_code.description: A short, predefined code that uniquely identifies the warning type.

warnings.description: A list of warnings generated during the API request. These are non-critical issues or recommendations that might affect the request but won't stop it from processing. Each warning object provides a message to inform the user of potential problems.

extension_data.description: Extension data for customer Api response information

description.description: A plain message offering helpful context about the response, such as confirmation of fallback logic or skipped operations.

information.description: Contains additional non-critical information relevant to the request or response. This field provides extra details that might assist in understanding the context of the API response but is not essential for processing.

schema.description:
Response for retrieving an article by URL.
Extends the standard article response with available_languages and settings.
schema.title: GetArticleByUrlResponse
